title: UltrafastSecp256k1: GPU-accelerated, audit-grade secp256k1 ECC library
description:
Ultra high-performance secp256k1 ECC library featuring GPU-accelerated ECDSA and Schnorr signatures (CUDA, Metal, OpenCL, ROCm), constant-time side-channel protection, 12+ platform targets, and zero external dependencies. Achieves 4.88M ECDSA signs/s on a single GPU.

Engineered for an audit-grade trust model: every release ships with a Continuous-Audit-as-Code (CAAS) evidence bundle, 189 exploit proof-of-concept regression tests, formal invariant specifications, multi-CI reproducible-build attestations across GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Woodpecker (Codeberg), and a documented INTEROP matrix cross-checked against published reference implementations (Wycheproof, libsecp256k1, OpenSSL libcrypto).

License: MIT. Repository: https://github.com/shrec/UltrafastSecp256k1.
references: Wuille, P. et al. BIP-340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1 (2020).
Bernstein, D. J. et al. RFC 6979: Deterministic Usage of DSA and ECDSA (2013).
Brown, D. R. L. SEC 2: Recommended Elliptic Curve Domain Parameters v2.0 (2010).
notes: Release archives published on Zenodo are byte-identical to the corresponding GitHub release tags and to the multi-CI reproducible-build attestations under artifacts/reproducible-attestation.json on each provider (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Woodpecker / Codeberg).
